mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, increasing present equity values and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns exhibit non-positive mean excess return following low FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 9.5% signals persistent labor market weakness that depresses consumer spending forecasts and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in retail sales and industrial production confirm a synchronized demand recovery that boosts revenue expectations across sectors, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint directional movement of RSAFS and INDPRO fails to achieve a Rank IC greater than 0.04 for 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPI inflation during a period of near-zero federal funds rates signals real monetary easing that reduces real debt burdens and stimulates nominal revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive CPIAUCSL changes combined with FEDFUNDS below 0.25% do not produce positive 60-day forward returns with Rank IC > 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output that supports corporate revenue growth and margin expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO do not correlate with positive 5-day S&P 500 returns (Rank IC < 0.03 over a 2-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts level below 600 thousand units signals a stalled construction sector that drags down aggregate demand and employment expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 55% of instances when HOUST < 600.0 over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate signal authentic labor market tightening that lifts consumer income and spending trajectories, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint movement of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to generate positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns with Rank IC > 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth coinciding with a federal funds rate below 0.5% indicates a demand recovery amplified by accommodative monetary policy, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 levels combined with FEDFUNDS below 0.5% do not produce 60-day forward returns significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year window.
